name: midnight-in-chernobyl
description: Adam Higginbotham's 'Midnight in Chernobyl: The Untold Story of the World's Greatest Nuclear Disaster' — the definitive account of the 1986 Chernobyl nuclear catastrophe, from the flawed RBMK reactor design and the fatal experiment that triggered the explosion, to the cover-up, the liquidators, the trial, and the long shadow of radiation poisoning.
useCase: Understanding the full story of the Chernobyl disaster, the RBMK reactor design flaws, the Soviet cover-up, the heroic fatalities of the firefighters and liquidators, and the political impact on the fall of the USSR.
knownRisks: Graphic descriptions of acute radiation syndrome — not for sensitive readers
Some details are reconstructed from testimony — not all facts are certain
The book is critical of Soviet leadership — political context matters
Dense with technical details about nuclear engineering
